forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative

forms.title: Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application
forms.notes: Online filing only via CEAC, for consular visa issuance. travel.state.gov returned 403 to automated fetch; verified via official-domain search results.

supporting_documents.notes: Cap-subject petitions only

supporting_documents.notes: Standard petition evidence

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: 09/17/18 edition remains accepted; USCIS states a new edition is coming.

forms.notes: For L-2 dependents extending or changing status in the US; I-539A supplement at https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-539a.pdf.

forms.title: Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application
forms.notes: Online filing only via CEAC, for consular visa issuance. travel.state.gov returned 403 to automated fetch; verified via official-domain search results.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of qualifying relationship between US and foreign entity (parent, branch, subsidiary, or affiliate)
Evidence of one continuous year of qualifying employment abroad within the preceding three years
Evidence the US role is executive or managerial capacity
supporting_documents.notes: Per INA 101(a)(44) definitions

supporting_documents.name: New office evidence (secured physical premises, financial ability to support the position within one year)
supporting_documents.notes: New-office petitions only

visas.description: Intracompany transferee entering to provide services in a specialized knowledge capacity for a qualifying organization.

forms.title: Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application
forms.notes: Online filing only via CEAC, for consular visa issuance. travel.state.gov returned 403 to automated fetch; verified via official-domain search results.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of qualifying relationship between US and foreign entity
Evidence of one continuous year of qualifying employment abroad within the preceding three years
Evidence the beneficiary has specialized knowledge and the US role requires it
supporting_documents.notes: Special knowledge of company product/processes or advanced knowledge of company procedures

supporting_documents.name: New office evidence (premises, financial ability)
supporting_documents.notes: New-office petitions only

visas.description: Individuals with extraordinary ability in sciences, education, business, or athletics, demonstrated by sustained national or international acclaim.

forms.title: Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker
forms.notes: Filed by a US employer or agent.

forms.title: O and P Classifications Supplement to Form I-129
forms.notes: Contained within the single Form I-129 PDF (begins page 28); not a separate download.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: 09/17/18 edition remains accepted; USCIS states a new edition is coming.

forms.title: Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status
forms.notes: For O-3 dependents extending or changing status in the US; I-539A supplement at https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-539a.pdf.

forms.title: Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application
forms.notes: Online filing only via CEAC, for consular visa issuance. travel.state.gov returned 403 to automated fetch; verified via official-domain search results.

supporting_documents.name: Consultation: written advisory opinion from a peer group (including labor organizations) or expert in the beneficiary's field
supporting_documents.notes: May be waived only if no appropriate peer group exists

supporting_documents.name: Copy of written contract between petitioner and beneficiary, or summary of the oral agreement terms
Itinerary: nature of events/activities with beginning and end dates for the validity period requested
supporting_documents.notes: Required where applicable, e.g. agent-filed or multi-venue engagements

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of extraordinary ability (major award or at least 3 of the O-1A regulatory criteria)
supporting_documents.notes: Awards, memberships, published material, judging, original contributions, scholarly articles, critical employment, high salary

supporting_documents.name: Agent documentation (if filed by a US agent)
supporting_documents.notes: Evidence of authority to act as agent

visas.description: Individuals with extraordinary ability in the arts or extraordinary achievement in the motion picture or television industry.

forms.title: Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker
forms.notes: Filed by a US employer or agent.

forms.title: O and P Classifications Supplement to Form I-129
forms.notes: Contained within the single Form I-129 PDF (begins page 28); not a separate download.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: 09/17/18 edition remains accepted; USCIS states a new edition is coming.

forms.title: Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status
forms.notes: For O-3 dependents extending or changing status in the US; I-539A supplement at https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-539a.pdf.

forms.title: Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application
forms.notes: Online filing only via CEAC, for consular visa issuance. travel.state.gov returned 403 to automated fetch; verified via official-domain search results.

supporting_documents.name: Consultation: advisory opinion from peer group or labor organization; for motion picture/TV, from both the labor union and a management organization
supporting_documents.notes: Dual consultation is specific to motion picture/TV

supporting_documents.name: Copy of written contract between petitioner and beneficiary, or summary of the oral agreement terms
Itinerary of events/activities with dates
supporting_documents.notes: Required where applicable

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of distinction in the arts or extraordinary achievement in motion picture/TV (significant award/nomination or at least 3 regulatory criteria)
Agent documentation (if filed by a US agent)

visas.description: USMCA professionals: qualified Canadian and Mexican citizens entering to work in designated professional occupations.

forms.title: Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker
forms.notes: Only for extension of stay or change of status inside the US (or optional employer pre-filing for Canadians). Canadians typically apply at a CBP port of entry with no I-129; Mexicans apply at a consulate with DS-160.

forms.title: Trade Agreement Supplement to Form I-129
forms.notes: Contained within the single Form I-129 PDF (begins page 11); not a separate download.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: 09/17/18 edition remains accepted; USCIS states a new edition is coming.

forms.title: Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status
forms.notes: For TD dependents extending or changing status in the US; I-539A supplement at https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-539a.pdf.

forms.title: Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application
forms.notes: Online only via CEAC. Required for Mexican citizens; Canadians are generally visa-exempt and apply at the port of entry.

supporting_documents.name: Proof of Canadian or Mexican citizenship
Employer letter detailing professional capacity, purpose of employment, length of stay, and educational qualifications
supporting_documents.notes: Presented at port of entry (Canadians) or consulate (Mexicans)

supporting_documents.name: Credentials evaluation of foreign degrees
supporting_documents.notes: If applicable

supporting_documents.name: Evidence the occupation is on the USMCA professional list and the beneficiary meets its qualifications
supporting_documents.notes: Per profession in USMCA Appendix 2

visas.description: Treaty investors (and qualifying employees) of a treaty country who have invested, or are investing, substantial capital in a US enterprise they direct and develop.

forms.title: Petition for a Nonimmigrant Worker
forms.notes: Only for change of status to E-2 or extension inside the US. Applicants abroad apply directly at a consulate (DS-160 plus DS-156E); no USCIS petition for consular processing.

forms.title: E-1/E-2 Classification Supplement to Form I-129
forms.notes: Contained within the single Form I-129 PDF (begins page 9); not a separate download.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: 09/17/18 edition remains accepted; USCIS states a new edition is coming.

forms.title: Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status
forms.notes: For E-2 dependent dependents extending or changing status in the US; I-539A supplement at https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-539a.pdf.

forms.title: Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application
forms.notes: Online only via CEAC; required of all E-2 consular applicants.

forms.title: Nonimmigrant Treaty Trader/Investor Application
forms.notes: Completed with DS-160 for E-1/E-2 executives, managers, and essential employees. Direct PDF links on eforms.state.gov returned 404 at verification time, so pdf_url is null; obtain via eforms.state.gov or the consulate.

supporting_documents.name: Proof of treaty-country nationality of the investor (and employer, for employee applicants)
Evidence of substantial investment of capital in a bona fide US enterprise
supporting_documents.notes: Funds committed and at risk; source and path of funds documentation

supporting_documents.name: Evidence the investor directs and develops the enterprise (at least 50% ownership or operational control)
Evidence the enterprise is not marginal (business plan, financial projections, payroll)
Evidence of intent to depart the US when E-2 status ends
For E-2 employees: evidence of executive/supervisory role or essential skills, and same treaty nationality as employer

visas.description: Employment-based first preference, extraordinary ability in sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics; self-petition allowed (no employer or labor certification needed).

forms.title: Immigrant Petition for Alien Workers
forms.notes: May be self-petitioned by the beneficiary; no job offer or labor certification required for EB-1A.

forms.title: Request for Premium Processing Service
forms.notes: I-140 is listed as premium-processing eligible on the I-907 page.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented by an attorney or accredited representative.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence meeting at least 3 of the 10 extraordinary-ability criteria, or evidence of a one-time achievement (e.g., Pulitzer, Oscar, Olympic Medal)
supporting_documents.notes: Criteria list published on the USCIS EB-1 page.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence the beneficiary will continue working in the area of expertise in the U.S.
Evidence of sustained national or international acclaim
supporting_documents.notes: Overall standard the criteria evidence must demonstrate.

visas.description: Employment-based first preference, outstanding professors and researchers with international recognition; requires a U.S. employer job offer but no labor certification.

forms.title: Immigrant Petition for Alien Workers
forms.notes: Filed by the U.S. employer; no labor certification required.

forms.title: Request for Premium Processing Service
forms.notes: I-140 is listed as premium-processing eligible on the I-907 page.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence meeting at least 2 of the 6 outstanding-researcher criteria
Evidence of at least 3 years of experience in teaching or research in the academic field
Offer of employment for a tenure/tenure-track teaching or comparable research position
supporting_documents.notes: From a university, institution of higher education, or qualifying private employer.

supporting_documents.name: For private employers: evidence of documented accomplishments and employment of at least 3 full-time researchers
supporting_documents.notes: Applies only when the petitioner is a private employer rather than a university.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of employer's ability to pay the offered wage
supporting_documents.notes: Standard initial evidence for employer-filed I-140 petitions (annual reports, federal tax returns, or audited financial statements per I-140 instructions).

visas.description: Employment-based first preference, certain multinational managers or executives transferring to a qualifying U.S. employer; no labor certification required.

forms.title: Immigrant Petition for Alien Workers
forms.notes: Filed by the U.S. employer, which must have been doing business for at least 1 year.

forms.title: Request for Premium Processing Service
forms.notes: I-140 is listed as premium-processing eligible on the I-907 page.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of at least 1 year of qualifying employment abroad within the 3 years preceding the petition (or most recent lawful nonimmigrant admission)
Evidence of qualifying relationship between the U.S. petitioner and the foreign entity
supporting_documents.notes: Parent/subsidiary/affiliate/branch relationship.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence the U.S. role and the prior foreign role are managerial or executive capacity
Evidence the U.S. petitioner has been doing business for at least 1 year
Evidence of employer's ability to pay the offered wage
supporting_documents.notes: Standard initial evidence for employer-filed I-140 petitions.

visas.description: Employment-based second preference with national interest waiver of the job offer and labor certification; self-petition allowed (no employer needed).

forms.title: Immigrant Petition for Alien Workers
forms.notes: May be self-petitioned; labor certification waived in the national interest.

forms.title: Application for Permanent Employment Certification
forms.notes: For NIW the I-140 must be accompanied by a completed Form ETA-9089 Appendix A and a signed ETA-9089 Final Determination (uncertified, not DOL-approved); the operational form is filed online via DOL FLAG, online filing only.

forms.title: Request for Premium Processing Service
forms.notes: I-140 is listed as premium-processing eligible on the I-907 page.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of advanced degree or exceptional ability (EB-2 underlying qualification)
supporting_documents.notes: Degree records/transcripts, or evidence meeting the exceptional-ability criteria.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence addressing the 3 national-interest factors (substantial merit and national importance; well positioned to advance the endeavor; beneficial to waive the job offer requirement)
supporting_documents.notes: USCIS considers these 3 factors; qualifying endeavors are not defined by statute.

supporting_documents.name: Completed ETA-9089 Appendix A and signed ETA-9089 Final Determination
supporting_documents.notes: Included in the I-140 package in lieu of a DOL-certified labor certification.

visas.description: Employment-based second preference for advanced-degree professionals or persons of exceptional ability, employer-sponsored with DOL PERM labor certification.

forms.title: Application for Permanent Employment Certification
forms.notes: Online filing only via DOL FLAG (for applications filed on or after June 1, 2023); requires a prevailing wage determination (ETA-9141) from the NPWC and pre-filing recruitment before submission; the approved and signed Final Determination accompanies the I-140.

forms.title: Immigrant Petition for Alien Workers
forms.notes: Filed by the U.S. employer after PERM approval.

forms.title: Request for Premium Processing Service
forms.notes: I-140 is listed as premium-processing eligible on the I-907 page.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented.

supporting_documents.name: Approved and signed ETA-9089 Final Determination (labor certification)
supporting_documents.notes: For FLAG-era filings, the Final Determination - Permanent Employment Certification Approval.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of advanced degree (degree plus transcripts) or evidence meeting the exceptional-ability criteria
supporting_documents.notes: Must also meet any education/experience requirements stated on the labor certification.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of employer's ability to pay the offered wage
supporting_documents.notes: Annual reports, federal tax returns, or audited financial statements per I-140 instructions.

visas.description: Employment-based third preference for skilled workers, professionals, and other workers, employer-sponsored with DOL PERM labor certification.

forms.title: Application for Permanent Employment Certification
forms.notes: Online filing only via DOL FLAG (for applications filed on or after June 1, 2023); requires a prevailing wage determination (ETA-9141) from the NPWC and pre-filing recruitment before submission.

forms.title: Immigrant Petition for Alien Workers
forms.notes: Filed by the U.S. employer after PERM approval.

forms.title: Request for Premium Processing Service
forms.notes: I-140 is listed as premium-processing eligible on the I-907 page.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented.

supporting_documents.name: Signed Form ETA-9089 approved by DOL (or, for FLAG filings on/after June 1, 2023, the approved and signed Final Determination - Permanent Employment Certification Approval)

Evidence of education, training, or experience matching the subcategory (skilled worker: 2+ years training/experience; professional: U.S. baccalaureate or foreign equivalent; other worker: capable of unskilled labor) and the job requirements on the labor certification
Evidence of employer's ability to pay the offered wage
supporting_documents.notes: Annual reports, federal tax returns, or audited financial statements per I-140 instructions.

visas.description: Employment-based fifth preference immigrant investor program: investment in a U.S. commercial enterprise creating or preserving 10 permanent full-time jobs for qualified U.S. workers.

forms.title: Immigrant Petition by Standalone Investor
forms.notes: For standalone (direct) investors not investing through a regional center.

forms.title: Immigrant Petition by Regional Center Investor
forms.notes: For investors pooling investment through a USCIS-designated regional center; file I-526E or I-526, not both.

forms.title: Petition by Investor to Remove Conditions on Permanent Resident Status
forms.notes: Second-stage petition to remove conditions on the 2-year conditional green card.

forms.title: Request for Premium Processing Service
forms.notes: NOT available for EB-5: the I-907 page lists only I-129, I-140, certain I-765 categories, and certain I-539 classifications as eligible; I-526, I-526E, and I-829 are not premium-processing eligible.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of the required capital investment in a U.S. commercial enterprise
supporting_documents.notes: Investment threshold depends on whether the project is in a targeted employment area; amounts set by the EB-5 Reform and Integrity Act of 2022.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of a plan to create or preserve 10 permanent full-time jobs for qualified U.S. workers
supporting_documents.notes: Business plan and job-creation evidence; for I-829, evidence the jobs were created/preserved.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of lawful source and path of invested funds
supporting_documents.notes: Per I-526/I-526E instructions (tax returns, bank records, transaction documentation); not itemized on the pages fetched.

supporting_documents.name: For I-526E: evidence of investment through a USCIS-designated regional center and the regional center's project documents
supporting_documents.notes: Applies to regional center investors only.

visas.description: Immediate-relative / spouse immigrant petition filed by a U.S. citizen or LPR petitioner (I-130 based); classification step preceding AOS or consular processing.

forms.title: Petition for Alien Relative
forms.notes: Instructions PDF at https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-130instr.pdf.

forms.title: Supplemental Information for a Spouse Beneficiary
forms.notes: Required whenever the beneficiary is a spouse; linked from the I-130 page. No standalone form page.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented; 09/17/18 and 05/23/18 editions currently accepted, new edition forthcoming per the page.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of petitioner's U.S. citizenship, LPR status, or U.S. national status (birth certificate, naturalization/citizenship certificate, FS-240 CRBA, unexpired U.S. passport, or front/back copy of Green Card)
supporting_documents.notes: One of the listed items

supporting_documents.name: Marriage certificate (copy)
supporting_documents.notes: For spouse petitions; other relatives use birth certificates establishing the relationship

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of termination of any prior marriages (divorce decree, annulment, death certificate)
supporting_documents.notes: Required if applicable

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of bona fides of the marriage (joint property, joint lease, combined finances, birth certificates of shared children, third-party affidavits)
supporting_documents.notes: For spouse petitions

supporting_documents.name: Proof of legal name change
supporting_documents.notes: If applicable

supporting_documents.name: Two passport-style photographs
supporting_documents.notes: If applicable per checklist

supporting_documents.name: Certified English translations of any foreign-language documents
supporting_documents.notes: Translator certification of completeness, accuracy, and competence

visas.description: Fiancé(e) of a U.S. citizen (I-129F based); K-1 nonimmigrant visa issued abroad, marriage required within 90 days of admission, then AOS.

forms.title: Petition for Alien Fiancé(e)
forms.notes: Also used for K-3 spouse classification.

forms.title: Online Nonimmigrant Visa Application
forms.notes: K visas are nonimmigrant visas; the beneficiary files DS-160 online via CEAC at the consular stage. Online only, no PDF. travel.state.gov returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch.

forms.title: Declaration of Financial Support
forms.notes: Commonly requested by consular officers at the K-1 interview; not the I-864 (that comes later at AOS).

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented; 09/17/18 and 05/23/18 editions currently accepted, new edition forthcoming per the page.

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of petitioner's U.S. citizenship (birth certificate, naturalization/citizenship certificate, FS-240 CRBA, unexpired U.S. passport, or consular officer statement)
supporting_documents.notes: K-1 petitioner must be a U.S. citizen

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of legal termination of any previous marriages (final divorce decree, annulment order, or death certificate)
supporting_documents.notes: Required if applicable, for either party

supporting_documents.name: One color passport-style photograph of petitioner and one of fiancé(e), taken within 30 days of filing
Evidence of bona fide intention to marry and intent to marry within 90 days of K-1 admission
Evidence of in-person meeting within 2 years of filing
supporting_documents.notes: Or evidence qualifying for the cultural-custom or extreme-hardship exemption

supporting_documents.name: Evidence supporting an IMBRA waiver
supporting_documents.notes: If applicable

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of legal name change
supporting_documents.notes: If applicable

supporting_documents.name: Certified English translations of any foreign-language documents

visas.description: Concurrent adjustment-of-status package filed inside the U.S.: I-485 plus affidavit of support, medical exam, and optional EAD/advance-parole companions.

forms.title: Application to Register Permanent Residence or Adjust Status
forms.notes: I-693 (or partial I-693) must be submitted with the I-485 or the package may be rejected. Supplement A applies for INA 245(i) cases. Current editions include the INA 213A exemption request formerly made on I-864W.

forms.title: Affidavit of Support Under Section 213A of the INA
forms.notes: Required for family-based cases. I-864EZ is an alternative for qualifying single-sponsor W-2 cases.

forms.title: Contract Between Sponsor and Household Member
forms.notes: One per household member whose income is used to qualify. PDF filename is capitalized 'I-864a.pdf' as linked on the page.

forms.title: Affidavit of Support Under Section 213A of the INA (EZ)
forms.notes: Simplified variant when the petitioning sponsor is the only sponsor, sponsors one immigrant, and qualifies on W-2 salary/pension income alone.

forms.title: Request for Exemption for Intending Immigrant's Affidavit of Support
forms.notes: Page is live and lists a 12/08/21 edition, but no form PDF is linked on the page at fetch time, so pdf_url is unverifiable. Current I-485 editions capture the 213A exemption request directly.

forms.title: Report of Immigration Medical Examination and Vaccination Record
forms.notes: Completed by a USCIS-designated civil surgeon; delivered in a sealed envelope; must be filed with the I-485. Civil-surgeon signatures on/after July 3, 2025 require the 01/20/25 edition.

forms.title: Application for Employment Authorization
forms.notes: Optional interim EAD under (c)(9) while the I-485 is pending; may be filed concurrently.

forms.title: Application for Travel Documents, Parole Documents, and Arrival/Departure Records
forms.notes: Optional advance parole so the applicant can travel without abandoning the pending I-485.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Only if represented; 09/17/18 and 05/23/18 editions currently accepted, new edition forthcoming per the page.

forms.title: Declaration of Self-Sufficiency
forms.notes: DISCONTINUED. Do not file: beginning March 9, 2021, applicants should not file Form I-944 or its evidence with Form I-485 (2019 public charge rule vacated). Recorded only to prevent inclusion.

forms.title: E-Notification of Application/Petition Acceptance
forms.notes: Referenced on the I-485 page (clip to first page for lockbox e-notification). Edition/pdf verified separately on the COMMON profile.

supporting_documents.name: Two passport-style photographs
Copy of government-issued identity document with photograph
Copy of applicant's birth certificate
supporting_documents.notes: If unavailable/nonexistent, other acceptable evidence of birth plus proof of unavailability

supporting_documents.name: Inspection and admission, or inspection and parole documentation (e.g., I-94)
supporting_documents.notes: Unless adjusting under INA 245(i)

supporting_documents.name: Documentation of immigrant category: Form I-797 approval or receipt notice for the I-130 filed on applicant's behalf
supporting_documents.notes: Unless the I-485 is filed concurrently with the I-130

supporting_documents.name: Form I-693 in sealed civil-surgeon envelope (or partial I-693 / vaccination record)
supporting_documents.notes: Must accompany the I-485 or the package may be rejected

supporting_documents.name: Form I-864 or I-864EZ affidavit of support with sponsor's federal tax return and W-2s for the most recent year (1099s, schedules; optionally 3 years of returns, 6 months of pay stubs, employer letter)
supporting_documents.notes: Self-employed sponsors include Schedule C/D/E/F; joint/household income requires I-864A per household member

supporting_documents.name: Certified police and court records of all criminal charges, arrests, or convictions regardless of disposition
supporting_documents.notes: If applicable

supporting_documents.name: Evidence of bona fide marriage entered within 90 days of K-1 admission
supporting_documents.notes: Required for K nonimmigrant adjustment applicants

supporting_documents.name: Waiver forms if inadmissible (I-601, I-212, I-612) and A/G/NATO forms (I-508, I-566) as applicable
supporting_documents.notes: Situational

supporting_documents.name: Certified English translations of any foreign-language documents

visas.description: Immigrant visa consular processing after I-130 approval: NVC stage (fees, DS-260, I-864, civil documents via CEAC) then embassy/consulate interview.

forms.title: Immigrant Visa Electronic Application (Application for Immigrant Visa and Alien Registration)
forms.notes: Online only, completed in CEAC after NVC fees are paid; no PDF exists. One per applicant including each accompanying family member. travel.state.gov returned HTTP 403 to direct automated fetch; confirmed via official-domain search results.

forms.title: Affidavit of Support Under Section 213A of the INA
forms.notes: USCIS form submitted to NVC (uploaded to CEAC) during consular processing with the sponsor's financial evidence.

forms.title: Contract Between Sponsor and Household Member
forms.notes: One per household member whose income is used to qualify. PDF filename is capitalized 'I-864a.pdf' as linked on the page.

forms.title: Affidavit of Support Under Section 213A of the INA (EZ)
forms.notes: Simplified variant when the petitioning sponsor is the only sponsor, sponsors one immigrant, and qualifies on W-2 salary/pension income alone.

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney or Accredited Representative
forms.notes: Covers representation in the USCIS petition stage. A separate G-28I exists for matters outside the U.S.

supporting_documents.name: Valid passport (biographic data page copy to NVC; original to interview)
Birth certificate (original or certified copy) for applicant and each immigrating family member
supporting_documents.notes: Photocopy uploaded to CEAC; originals brought to interview

supporting_documents.name: Marriage certificate(s) for every marriage, plus divorce/annulment/death certificates terminating prior marriages
Police certificates from all countries of residence (applicants 16 or older)
supporting_documents.notes: U.S. police certificates are not required for present/former U.S. residents

supporting_documents.name: Court and prison records; military records
supporting_documents.notes: If applicable

supporting_documents.name: Photographs per DOS immigrant-visa specification
supporting_documents.notes: Brought to the visa interview

supporting_documents.name: Sponsor financial evidence for I-864: federal tax return and W-2s for most recent year (1099s/schedules; optionally 3 years, pay stubs, employer letter)
Medical examination by a DOS-authorized panel physician
supporting_documents.notes: Consular cases use the panel-physician exam, not USCIS Form I-693 (domestic civil-surgeon form for AOS)

supporting_documents.name: Certified translations of documents not in English or the official language of the country of application

visas.description: Naturalization to U.S. citizenship for eligible lawful permanent residents

forms.title: Application for Naturalization
forms.notes: Instructions PDF also linked on page: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/n-400instr.pdf. Can be filed online.

supporting_documents.name: Copy of Permanent Resident Card (Green Card)
supporting_documents.notes: Listed in the page's Checklist of Required Initial Evidence

supporting_documents.name: Current marriage certificate and evidence of spouse's U.S. citizenship for the last 3 years (birth certificate, naturalization/citizenship certificate, U.S. passport biographical page, or consular report)
supporting_documents.notes: Required only when applying under the 3-year rule based on marriage to a U.S. citizen

supporting_documents.name: Divorce decree, annulment decree, or death certificate terminating any prior marriages
supporting_documents.notes: Required if applicant or spouse had prior marriages

supporting_documents.name: Two passport-style photographs
supporting_documents.notes: Only if residing outside the United States; photographs otherwise no longer required

supporting_documents.name: Form N-648, Medical Certification for Disability Exceptions
supporting_documents.notes: Only when requesting an exception to the English and/or civics tests

visas.description: Request for a USCIS officer hearing on a denied naturalization application

forms.title: Request for a Hearing on a Decision in Naturalization Proceedings (Under Section 336 of the INA)
forms.notes: Instructions PDF also linked on page: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/n-336instr.pdf

visas.description: Certificate of citizenship for persons who acquired or derived U.S. citizenship through parents

forms.title: Application for Certificate of Citizenship
forms.notes: Instructions PDF also linked on page: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/n-600instr.pdf. Can be filed online.

visas.description: Replacement of a lost, mutilated, or incorrect Certificate of Naturalization or Citizenship

forms.title: Application for Replacement Naturalization/Citizenship Document
forms.notes: Instructions PDF also linked on page: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/n-565instr.pdf. Original document mailed to the Nebraska Service Center when applicable.

supporting_documents.name: Original document or certificate being replaced
supporting_documents.notes: Required per the page checklist if applying for a new or replacement document on the basis of the original (e.g. mutilated or incorrect)

supporting_documents.name: Two identical passport-style photographs
supporting_documents.notes: Only if the applicant lives outside the United States

visas.description: Forms attached across many immigration filings

forms.notes: Instructions PDF: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/g-28instr.pdf

forms.title: Notice of Entry of Appearance as Attorney in Matters Outside the Geographical Confines of the United States
forms.notes: Instructions PDF: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/g-28iinstr.pdf

forms.title: E-Notification of Application/Petition Acceptance
forms.notes: No separate instructions PDF linked on the page

forms.title: Request for Premium Processing Service
forms.notes: Instructions PDF: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-907instr.pdf

forms.title: Application to Extend/Change Nonimmigrant Status
forms.notes: Supplement I-539A for co-applicants also linked on page (https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-539a.pdf); instructions: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-539instr.pdf

forms.title: Application for Action on an Approved Application or Petition
forms.notes: Instructions PDF: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-824instr.pdf

forms.title: Application to Replace Permanent Resident Card (Green Card)
forms.notes: Instructions PDF: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-90instr.pdf

forms.title: Application for Employment Authorization
forms.notes: Worksheet I-765WS also linked on page (https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-765ws.pdf); instructions: https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/forms/i-765instr.pdf

forms.title: Application for Travel Documents, Parole Documents, and Arrival/Departure Records
